steps.title: Find the true subject
steps.content: The sentence opens "The artistic talents of Barbara Chase-Riboud..." The head noun is talents — plural. The prepositional phrase "of Barbara Chase-Riboud" is not the subject and does not affect agreement. The blank needs a plural verb.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• "hasn't been": singular contraction ("has not been"); does not agree with the plural subject talents. ✗
  • "wasn't": singular past tense ("was not"); does not agree with the plural subject talents. ✗
  • "isn't": singular present tense ("is not"); does not agree with the plural subject talents. ✗
  • "aren't": plural present tense ("are not"); agrees with the plural subject talents. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
